rkagi 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • rkagi Quickstart Guide

Construct summarize results object

new_kagi_summarize_results.Rd

Build a typed S3 object of class kagi_summarize_results from a successful Universal Summarizer response. The object keeps both the raw JSON payload (as returned by the API) and the parsed fields you will typically use (meta, summary, tokens).

Usage

new_kagi_summarize_results(request, raw_json, parsed)

Arguments

request

A prepared summarize request of class kagi_summarize (the object you passed to the API).

raw_json

A length-one character string containing the raw JSON response body returned by the API.

parsed

A parsed list (e.g. from jsonlite::fromJSON(..., simplifyVector = FALSE)) expected to contain top-level elements meta and data. From data, the function extracts output (as summary) and tokens.

Value

An object of class kagi_summarize_results with components:

request

The original kagi_summarize request.

raw_json

Raw JSON string for reproducibility/debugging.

meta

List with response metadata (e.g., ms, node, api_balance).

summary

Character scalar with the summarizer output text (or list() if absent).

tokens

Integer (or list() if absent) with token usage reported by the API.

Details

This is a low-level constructor; most users will obtain a kagi_summarize_results via kagi_perform() (or your high-level wrapper). The fields use %||% to provide safe fallbacks when the API omits optional elements.

See also

kagi_perform, new_kagi_summarize (request constructor),

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
parsed <- list(
  meta = list(ms = 123, node = "xyz", api_balance = 42),
  data = list(output = "Short summary...", tokens = 256L)
)
req <- new_kagi_summarize(new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "summarize"), text = "Lorem ipsum")
res <- new_kagi_summarize_results(req, raw_json = jsonlite::toJSON(parsed), parsed = parsed)
res
} # }
